title: Davide Studios: Monthly Newsletter — June Issue
preheader: A June edit of London exhibitions, fashion image-making and studio-adjacent notes from Davide Studios.

research.notes: All listed stories were checked against official museum, gallery, organiser or brand sources on 28 June 2026. External campaign and exhibition visuals are referenced from official pages; reusable press permissions should still be confirmed before a live audience send.

openingNote: A June edit built around images that gather force in public: Pan-African art at the Barbican, Japanese women photographers in Soho, portraiture under close pressure, and fashion campaigns that use summer light, city rhythm and set construction as part of the visual argument.

art.label: 01 — Art
art.intro: A London-facing edit for photography, portraiture, contemporary art and image culture.

featured.title: Project a Black Planet: The Art and Culture of Panafrica
featured.location: Level 3, Barbican Centre, Silk Street, London EC2Y 8DS
featured.description: Barbican’s exhibition follows Pan-Africanism as a visual, political and cultural force from the 1920s to the present. More than 300 works move across painting, installation, poster, journal and film, with artists including Chris Ofili, Marlene Dumas, Claudette Johnson and Liz Johnson Artur.
featured.whyItMatters: It treats image-making as a shared geography of rupture, archive, solidarity and imagination, with photography sitting beside posters, journals, painting and film.

image.label: Official Barbican exhibition image
image.alt: Installation view of Project a Black Planet at Barbican Art Gallery
image.credit: Project a Black Planet installation view, 11 June - 6 September 2026. Image: Thomas Adank, Barbican Art Gallery.

items.title: Japanese Women Photographers: From 1950s to Now
items.description: The Photographers' Gallery places 27 Japanese women artists across post-war, contemporary and experimental practice, with more than 200 photographs, videos, installations and rare photobooks. The exhibition, organised with Aperture and Rencontres d'Arles, rewrites a history often told through a narrower lens.
items.whyItMatters: It opens a wide photographic field around authorship, social change, intimacy, colour, publication and the politics of who gets to define a national visual history.

items.title: Marilyn Monroe: A Portrait
items.location: Floor 0, National Portrait Gallery, St Martin's Place, London WC2H 0HE
items.description: Built around Monroe's centenary, this exhibition studies a public image made through collaboration with photographers, artists and stylists. The National Portrait Gallery foregrounds agency as much as iconography, moving from early pin-ups to Warhol, Boty, Avedon, Beaton, Eve Arnold and Sam Shaw.
items.whyItMatters: It is a compact study in how celebrity portraiture is built through gaze, repetition, authorship, styling and the delicate politics of being looked at.

items.title: M.C. Escher. The Exhibition
items.location: Embankment Galleries, South Wing, Somerset House, Strand, London WC2R 1LA
items.description: Somerset House brings M.C. Escher to London in a major summer presentation co-produced by Arthemisia and Fever. The exhibition foregrounds impossible structures, spatial illusion, repeat pattern and graphic precision, making it useful for anyone studying how composition can make logic feel unstable.
items.whyItMatters: Escher's visual language sharpens questions of pattern, perspective, optical tension and the moment a flat image begins to behave like architecture.

items.title: Herbert Smith Freehills Kramer Portrait Award 2026
items.location: Floor 2, National Portrait Gallery, St Martin's Place, London WC2H 0HE
items.description: The National Portrait Gallery's annual portrait award gathers contemporary painted portraits selected from international submissions. The 2026 display offers a useful counterpoint to photography: close observation, likeness, surface and sitter psychology slowed down through paint rather than captured through lens time.
items.whyItMatters: For photographers, painted portraiture sharpens questions of pose, scale, intimacy, expression and what a face can hold after the instant has passed.

fashion.label: 02 — Fashion
fashion.intro: A compact visual edit of campaigns, launches and creative direction selected for image-making rather than product noise.

stories.title: Originals are the Real Thing
stories.commentary: The adidas Originals and Coca-Cola campaign turns football anticipation into a summer street scene: a bus, a group of characters, red-and-white graphics and 2000s sportswear energy. The styling works because it keeps the collection close to movement, heat, public space and collective memory rather than presenting it as isolated product.
stories.imageCredit: Official adidas Originals x Coca-Cola press image from adidas News.

image.label: Official adidas Originals x Coca-Cola campaign image
image.alt: adidas Originals and Coca-Cola campaign image showing a group gathered around a red bus

stories.brand: H&M Move, H&M Home and Solar Club
stories.title: A collection of movement and togetherness
stories.commentary: H&M Group's Solar Club collaboration brings movement, table culture and coastal light into one visual world. The campaign imagery shifts between bodies in motion and bodies gathered around food, which makes the collection feel less like an activewear drop and more like a study of rhythm, leisure and communal styling.
stories.imageCredit: Official H&M Group campaign image for H&M Move, H&M Home and Solar Club.

image.label: Official H&M Group campaign image
image.alt: H&M Move, H&M Home and Solar Club campaign image showing runners and a coastal table scene

stories.title: A New York Minute
stories.commentary: Balenciaga's campaign is directed by Celine Song and built as three sixty-second Manhattan films around Sarah Pidgeon, Fall 26 pieces and the Le City, Le 7 Bowling and Rodeo bags. Its best idea is structural: it lets the city, film crew and behind-the-scenes mechanics become part of the fashion image.
stories.imageCredit: Official Balenciaga campaign page asset from the brand page; campaign directed by Celine Song.

image.label: Official Balenciaga campaign page image
image.alt: Balenciaga Le City bag image from the official A New York Minute campaign page

onTheField.label: 03 — On the Field
onTheField.intro: Studio-facing notes from the live creative ecosystem around Davide Studios.
onTheField.note: A rotating image from the Davide Studios website archive, chosen each month to keep the studio note alive without inventing diary entries or partnership news.

imageRotation.notes: The renderer selects one image from this website-image pool using the issue ID, so the visual rotates automatically for each monthly issue.
